mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates lower discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of autos and light trucks and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive monthly S&P 500 returns during supply-constrained expansions.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above threshold are followed by positive returns in >70% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rate confirms broad labor market deterioration, signaling imminent recession and forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Divergent labor signals show no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that sustains corporate profit margins, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns regardless of short-term market noise.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals produce negative forward returns in >55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in industrial production exceeding two percent indicates severe supply-side contraction and falling business investment, driving negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines above threshold are followed by positive returns in >60% of cases.
